name: Dubai
localTips: Get a red Nol card (or the Nol app) at any metro station — it covers metro, tram, buses and even the one-dirham abra boats across the Creek.
Taxis are metered and honest, but Careem often beats them on price — never accept an unmetered 'fixed fare' from the rank.
Friday brunch is the social institution, but note the weekend is Saturday–Sunday and Friday mornings are quiet until midday prayers finish.
Dress is relaxed in malls and beach areas, but carry a scarf or sleeves for mosques, souks and Old Dubai — and go easy on public displays of affection.
In the souks, opening prices are theatre: counter at a third, walk away once, and settle around half. Pay cash for the best deals.

activities.title: Al Fahidi Historical Neighbourhood
activities.description: Wander the coral-stone lanes and wind towers of Bastakiya, Dubai's last 19th-century quarter — art spaces and courtyard cafés hide behind carved doors.

activities.title: One-dirham abra across the Creek
activities.description: Squeeze onto a wooden abra with commuters and cross Dubai Creek between Bur Dubai and Deira — the city's oldest journey for one dirham.

activities.title: Deira Gold & Spice Souks
activities.description: Window-shop kilo-heavy necklaces in the covered Gold Souk, then follow the saffron and frankincense smells into the Spice Souk next door.

activities.title: Dubai Fountain show at Burj Lake
activities.description: Jets dance 150 metres high to Arabic pop and opera every 30 minutes from 6pm, with the lit Burj Khalifa behind — grab a spot on the Souk Al Bahar bridge.

activities.title: Dubai Mall aquarium wall & waterfall
activities.description: The world's biggest mall is an attraction in itself — the free three-storey aquarium viewing wall, the diver-sculpture waterfall and the Chinatown food street.

activities.title: JBR Beach & The Walk
activities.description: Two kilometres of public beach backed by The Walk's cafés and street art, with the Ain Dubai wheel looming across the water at Bluewaters.

activities.title: Al Seef heritage waterfront
activities.description: A lantern-lit recreation of old Dubai along the Creek — barasti-roofed lanes, dhows at anchor and floating cafés, best after sunset.

activities.title: Kite Beach & food trucks
activities.description: Dubai's most active public beach — kitesurfers, a 14km running track, volleyball nets and the famous SALT burger truck, all with a Burj Al Arab backdrop.

activities.title: Alserkal Avenue galleries
activities.description: Warehouses in industrial Al Quoz turned into the Gulf's best contemporary art district — a dozen free galleries plus specialty coffee at Nightjar.

activities.title: Ras Al Khor flamingo hides
activities.description: Free bird hides on the edge of the city where hundreds of greater flamingos feed against a skyline backdrop — bring binoculars or use the free scopes.

activities.title: Palm Jumeirah boardwalk
activities.description: Walk the crescent boardwalk past Atlantis with open Gulf on one side and the Marina skyline on the other — sunset turns the whole strip gold.

activities.title: Dubai Marina Walk after dark
activities.description: A seven-kilometre promenade beneath lit-up supertalls — superyachts, shisha terraces and the Cayan Tower's 90-degree twist reflected in the water.

activities.title: IMAGINE light show at Festival City
activities.description: A record-setting free water, fire and laser show projected onto a building-sized screen over Dubai Creek — shows run nightly at Festival Bay.

activities.title: Global Village pavilions
activities.description: Ninety countries' worth of street food, souvenirs and stage shows in one vast winter-season park — come hungry and stay for the fireworks.

activities.title: Arabian Tea House courtyard
activities.description: An Emirati breakfast institution in a turquoise-shuttered Al Fahidi courtyard — balaleet, chebab pancakes and regag bread under the vines.
activities.insiderNote: Order the Emirati breakfast tray for two and ask for karak instead of plain tea — and come before 10am to beat the tour groups.

activities.title: Ravi Restaurant in Satwa
activities.description: The Pakistani canteen that fed Dubai's builders since 1978 and earned its own adidas sneaker — mutton peshawari and fresh tandoor roti at plastic tables.
activities.insiderNote: It runs from early morning to around 3am, so it's the classic post-night-out stop — order chicken biryani, dal fry and a mango juice for under AED 30.

activities.title: Karak chai at a roadside cafeteria
activities.description: The emirate's real national drink: sweet, cardamom-heavy karak from old-school cafeterias like Filli in Al Mamzar — flash your lights and they bring it to the car.
activities.insiderNote: A cup costs one or two dirhams at neighbourhood cafeterias; order 'karak zafran' at Filli for the saffron version locals swear by.

activities.title: Waterfront Market fish auction
activities.description: Deira's cavernous modern fish market — hammour, sheri and tuna piled on ice, with butchers who'll clean your pick and grill houses that cook it.
activities.insiderNote: Buy your fish downstairs, then carry it to one of the small kitchens at the side that will fry or grill it with rice for a few dirhams per kilo.

activities.title: Al Qudra desert lakes
activities.description: Man-made lakes deep in the Saih Al Salam desert where oryx and gazelle drink at dusk — locals bring barbecues and stay for the Milky Way.
activities.insiderNote: Stop at the Last Exit food-truck park on the D63 for supplies, and park at Love Lake for the best sunset dune ridge — no permits needed.

activities.title: Deira dhow wharfage
activities.description: Wooden trading dhows bound for Iran and Somalia still load fridges and tyres along the Creek — a working port scene unchanged for decades.
activities.insiderNote: Walk the stretch between the abra stations around late afternoon when crews brew tea on deck — most are happy to chat and wave you aboard for photos.

activities.title: 3 Fils at Jumeirah Fishing Harbour
activities.description: A tiny no-reservations counter by the fishing boats that became one of MENA's best restaurants — Asian-influenced plates without the five-star markup.
activities.insiderNote: It doesn't take bookings: arrive at 5:30pm opening or put your name down and walk the harbour wall. The salmon tataki and wagyu sandos are the orders.

activities.title: Coffee Museum in Al Fahidi
activities.description: A wind-tower house stuffed with centuries of roasters, dallahs and grinders — watch Ethiopian and Emirati brewing ceremonies, then taste the results.
activities.insiderNote: The upstairs majlis room brews traditional gahwa with dates for a few dirhams — most visitors never climb the stairs to find it.

activities.title: Burj Khalifa At the Top
activities.description: The 828-metre needle's observation decks on floors 124–125 — the desert, the Gulf and the whole toy-town city from the world's tallest building.
activities.bookingQuery: Burj Khalifa At the Top observation deck ticket Dubai

activities.title: Museum of the Future
activities.description: The torus of calligraphy on Sheikh Zayed Road — five floors of 2071 space stations, DNA libraries and wellness tech. Slots sell out days ahead.
activities.bookingQuery: Museum of the Future ticket Dubai

activities.title: Dubai Frame sky bridge
activities.description: A 150-metre golden picture frame with a glass-floored bridge — old Deira on one side, new Downtown on the other, the whole point of Dubai in one view.

activities.title: Desert safari with Bedouin camp dinner
activities.description: Dune-bash the Lahbab red sands, then camel rides, falconry and barbecue under the stars at a desert camp — the classic Dubai evening out.
activities.bookingQuery: desert safari dune bashing BBQ dinner Dubai

activities.title: Dhow dinner cruise
activities.description: Dinner on a traditional wooden dhow gliding past the lit towers of the Marina or the old Creek — buffet, oud music and skyline photos from the water.

activities.title: La Perle by Dragone aqua show
activities.description: Ninety minutes of aerial stunts, motorbikes and divers plunging into a 2.7-million-litre on-stage pool — Dubai's resident Cirque-style spectacular.
activities.bookingQuery: La Perle by Dragone show ticket Dubai

activities.title: Aquaventure waterpark at Atlantis
activities.description: The world's largest waterpark — the Leap of Faith drop through a shark lagoon, a private beach and endless slides off the Palm's crescent.

activities.title: Sunrise hot air balloon over the desert
activities.description: Lift off before dawn and drift over the Margham dunes as gazelles scatter below and the sun climbs behind the Hajar Mountains.
activities.bookingQuery: sunrise hot air balloon ride Dubai desert

activities.title: Abu Dhabi day trip: Grand Mosque & Louvre
activities.description: Ninety minutes down the coast to Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque's 82 white domes and the rain-of-light dome of the Louvre Abu Dhabi.
activities.bookingQuery: Abu Dhabi day trip Sheikh Zayed Mosque from Dubai

activities.title: Jumeirah Mosque guided visit
activities.description: The 'Open Doors, Open Minds' morning tour inside Dubai's most beautiful mosque — an unhurried hour of questions over Emirati coffee and luqaimat.
